rationale: The citing paper centers on parenting motivation, time crunch, and within-category variety seeking in routine product choices, while the cited paper focuses on political identity effects on parents’ supplemental education program preferences (self-focus and need for structure), indicating only a broad thematic overlap in parent decision-making. With only a single DOI-level reference match and no in-text citation context provided, there is insufficient evidence to confidently treat this as clearly incidental or clearly substantive.
